description: Effective-policy fallback to cluster weights. Closes ROADMAP P2 (added in commit aa83664). New `bucket.PlacementMode` field ('weighted' default | 'strict') + `placement.EffectivePolicy` helper that tries bucket Placement first (excluding draining), falls back to synthesised cluster-weights policy when mode='weighted'. PUT routing + rebalance worker classifier use EffectivePolicy. stuck_single_policy fires ONLY for strict-flagged buckets. BulkPlacementFixDialog filters to strict-stuck only + offers 'Flip to weighted' shortcut. BucketDetail Placement tab gains Strict toggle with explainer + small 'strict' badge on compliance buckets. 6 stories.

userStories.title: bucket.PlacementMode field + meta wire shape + admin API
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a per-bucket PlacementMode field so the rebalance + PUT-routing paths can distinguish strict (compliance) buckets from weighted (auto-fallback) buckets.
acceptanceCriteria: Add `PlacementMode string` field to `meta.Bucket` with `json:"placement_mode,omitempty"` tag; legal values `""` (== weighted per default), `"weighted"`, `"strict"`
Validation helper `meta.ValidatePlacementMode(s string) error` accepts empty/weighted/strict; rejects others with new sentinel `ErrInvalidPlacementMode`
Admin API `PUT /admin/v1/buckets/{name}/placement` body extended with `mode: "weighted"|"strict"` (optional — absence = "weighted")
`GET /admin/v1/buckets/{name}/placement` response carries `mode` field always (computed: empty/NULL → "weighted")

`meta.Store` SetBucketPlacement signature extended (or atomic placement+mode PUT helper); contract test `casePlacementMode` covers read/write of all three values across memory + Cassandra + TiKV backends
Backwards-compat: rows without `placement_mode` column read as "weighted"
Cassandra: `ALTER TABLE buckets ADD placement_mode text` (idempotent via existing `alterStatements` helper that swallows `column already exists`)
TiKV: extend Bucket JSON blob encoding with the new field (schema-additive — old blobs decode mode as "")
Memory: extend in-memory struct field
Audit row stamped via `s3api.SetAuditOverride(ctx, "admin:UpdateBucketPlacementMode", "bucket:<name>", ...)` when mode changes
OpenAPI spec at `internal/adminapi/openapi.yaml` updated: PUT placement body schema gains `mode` field with enum constraint + GET response carries it
Unit tests on validation path (empty, weighted, strict, invalid)
`go vet ./...` passes
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Landed: meta.Bucket.PlacementMode + ValidatePlacementMode + ErrInvalidPlacementMode + NormalizePlacementMode; SetBucketPlacementMode on all 3 backends (cassandra LWT + ALTER TABLE buckets ADD placement_mode text, tikv updateBucket, memory in-struct); GetBucket/ListBuckets SELECT extended on cassandra; admin API PUT/GET extended (Mode field, audit action admin:UpdateBucketPlacementMode when present); OpenAPI updated; contract test casePlacementMode + ValidatePlacementMode unit tests.

userStories.title: placement.EffectivePolicy helper
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a single helper that resolves the effective routing policy from (bucket.Placement, bucket.PlacementMode, cluster.weights, clusterStates) so both PUT routing AND rebalance worker classifier share the same logic.
acceptanceCriteria: New helper `placement.EffectivePolicy(bucketPolicy map[string]int, mode string, clusterWeights map[string]int, clusterStates map[string]meta.ClusterStateRow) map[string]int` in `internal/data/placement/router.go` (or new file)
Logic step 1: compute `liveBucketPolicy` = `bucketPolicy` with entries removed where `clusterStates[id].State ∈ {draining_readonly, evacuating, pending, removed}` (any state ≠ live)
Logic step 2: if `len(liveBucketPolicy) > 0` → return liveBucketPolicy (bucket Placement still has at least one live target)
Logic step 3: if `len(liveBucketPolicy) == 0` AND `mode == "strict"` → return empty (caller falls back to class env spec.Cluster; strict-refuse semantic preserved)

Logic step 4: if `len(liveBucketPolicy) == 0` AND `mode != "strict"` (i.e. "" or "weighted") → return synthesised `cluster.weights` filtered to live + weight>0 (matches existing `placement.DefaultPolicy` semantic from cluster-weights cycle)
Logic step 5: if both empty → return empty (genuine no-target case)
Treat `mode == ""` as equivalent to `"weighted"` (backwards-compat default for legacy buckets)

Unit tests covering ALL branches: nil bucketPolicy + weighted (returns cluster.weights); nil bucketPolicy + strict (treated as weighted — strict requires explicit policy); bucketPolicy with all live; bucketPolicy with mixed live/draining; bucketPolicy all draining + weighted (fallback to weights); bucketPolicy all draining + strict (returns empty); all clusters drained both ways
Distribution test: `bucket.Placement={X:1}`, X draining, mode=weighted, `cluster.weights={Y:50,Z:50}` + 1000 PUTs → ~50/50 split on Y/Z (5% tolerance)
Distribution test: same setup but `mode=strict` → all 1000 PUTs return empty → caller observes 503 path
`go vet ./...` passes
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Landed: placement.EffectivePolicy(bucketPolicy, mode, clusterWeights, clusterStates) in internal/data/placement/policy.go alongside DefaultPolicy. Shared liveSubset helper filters policy entries to (cluster live OR absent from clusterStates per absence==live semantic) AND weight>0. Logic per PRD truth table: bucketPolicy non-empty after live filter → return as-is; empty+strict (with non-empty original) → nil (compliance refuse); otherwise → fallback to liveSubset(clusterWeights). mode=="" coerced to weighted via meta.NormalizePlacementMode. nil bucketPolicy + strict treated as weighted (strict needs explicit policy to be meaningful). 14 unit tests in effective_test.go: nil+weighted, nil+strict, empty-mode, all-live (all three modes), mixed live/draining, all-draining+weighted, all-draining+strict, all-clusters-drained, pending/removed excluded, absent-row-is-live, both-empty, zero-weight filtered, plus the two PRD distribution tests (1000 PUTs ~50/50 on Y/Z weighted-fallback; 1000 strict returns empty). go vet + go test ./internal/data/placement/ pass; racetest oracle TestRunCleanRunNoInconsistencies is the known flake from US-001 (passes in isolation).

userStories.title: PutChunks routing + rebalance worker classifier use EffectivePolicy
userStories.description: As a developer, I need PutChunks AND the rebalance worker scan to share the EffectivePolicy semantic so categorization matches actual routing behavior.
acceptanceCriteria: `internal/data/rados/Backend.PutChunks` (and S3 symmetric path) replaces direct `placement.PickClusterExcluding` call with `EffectivePolicy` lookup: reads `bucket.PlacementMode` via ctx (new `data.WithPlacementMode` helper) or alongside bucket; resolves cluster weights + states from in-process cache; calls `EffectivePolicy(...)` and uses returned map as picker input
When `EffectivePolicy` returns empty → falls back to `spec.Cluster` (class env) → may strict-refuse 503 (existing semantic)
Rebalance worker `internal/rebalance/worker.go::scanDistribution` replaces direct `PickClusterExcluding` with `EffectivePolicy` lookup for each (bucket, chunk) pair

Classifier updated: **migratable** = `EffectivePolicy` non-empty AND verdict cluster ≠ chunk.Cluster; **stuck_single_policy** = `EffectivePolicy` empty AND `mode == "strict"` AND bucket.Placement references only draining clusters; **stuck_no_policy** = `EffectivePolicy` empty AND (mode == "weighted" OR mode == "") AND ALL live clusters drained (genuinely no target)

Suggested policies in `by_bucket` entries for stuck_single_policy gain new field `placement_mode_override: "weighted"|"strict"` per suggestion: one suggestion offers `mode="weighted"` (flip to fallback), one offers `mode="strict"` with replacement Placement; UI renders both
Suggested policies for stuck_no_policy unchanged (already cover the cluster-weights-empty case)
Unit tests: bucket fixtures covering all 7 truth-table rows from PRD; classifier outputs match

Integration test (multi-cluster lab): seed buckets A/B/C/D from PRD walkthrough; drain cephb evacuate; assert /drain-impact reports stuck_single_policy=1 (bucket-C only — strict-flagged), migratable includes A+B+D, stuck_no_policy=0; PUT new chunks → land per EffectivePolicy verdict (B → default, A → either, D → cluster weights default)
`go vet ./...` passes
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Landed: PutChunks routing + rebalance worker classifier share placement.EffectivePolicy via two new ctx helpers (data.WithPlacementMode for the bucket mode string, placement.WithClusterStates for the meta-typed cluster_state snapshot — the latter lives in internal/data/placement because internal/data must not import internal/meta per the cycle constraint). Exposed placement.LiveSubset so backends can apply the same live-state filter to bucket Placement and cluster.weights without re-implementing the predicate. rados.Backend.PutChunks resolves activePolicy via LiveSubset(bucket) → strict-refuse (nil) when bucket non-empty + all-drained + mode=strict (falls to spec.Cluster which strict-refuses if drained) → spec.ClusterPinned short-circuit → LiveSubset(weights) auto-fallback. s3.Backend.clusterForPlacement mirrors the same logic minus ClusterPinned (s3 has no pin flag). ClassifyBucket signature flipped: (raw, effective, mode) string; migratable when effective non-empty, stuck_single_policy when effective empty + mode=strict + raw non-empty, stuck_no_policy otherwise. rebalance.scanDistribution computes EffectivePolicy once per bucket from (raw, mode, weights, states) and passes that as the picker input + classifier input — categorisation now matches actual routing. adminapi.computeDrainImpact synthesises a preview-states map (excludeForTargets clusters forced to draining_readonly) and calls EffectivePolicy + new ClassifyBucket per bucket. suggestedPoliciesForBucket(current, mode, category, exclude, live): strict-stuck buckets get a Flip to weighted suggestion at index 0 (PlacementModeOverride=weighted, preserves policy) + uniform + per-cluster strict replacements stamped PlacementModeOverride=strict. SuggestedPolicy.PlacementModeOverride field added with json tag + OpenAPI enum ["",weighted,strict]. Tests updated: TestClassifyBucketCovers exercises the new 6-row mode-aware truth table; TestWorkerCategorizesChunksAcrossBuckets tags bucket B mode=strict so stuck_single_policy=3 holds; TestClusterDrainImpact_CategorizesAndSorts tags b-stuck-single strict + checks index-0 is the flip-to-weighted suggestion; new TestSuggestedPoliciesForBucket_StrictStuckOffersFlipAndStrictReplacements + two new s3 backend tests (TestPutChunksWeightedAutoFallback / TestPutChunksStrictRefusesWeightedFallback) cover the strict-vs-weighted PUT-path divergence. go vet + go test ./... pass.

userStories.title: BucketDetail Placement tab gains 'Strict placement' toggle + strict badge
userStories.description: As an operator, I want a per-bucket toggle on the Placement tab so I can opt this bucket into strict mode for compliance/data-sovereignty reasons.
acceptanceCriteria: New `<Switch>` (or `<Toggle>` per existing UI primitive) control in BucketDetail Placement tab (`web/src/components/bucket/PlacementTab.tsx` or actual path; verify) labeled `Strict placement`
Default state: off (= mode "weighted")

Tooltip on toggle: `When ON, this bucket will refuse PUTs and block drain if all clusters in its Placement policy are draining. When OFF (default), it falls back to cluster default routing weights. Turn ON for compliance-sensitive buckets (data-sovereignty, replication design).`
Save action on Placement tab now sends `mode: "weighted"|"strict"` in PUT body (US-001 surface)
Visual indicator: strict-flagged buckets get small `<Badge>` reading `strict` in BucketDetail page header (next to bucket name)
When operator flips toggle ON, a confirmation prompt appears: `Strict placement may block drain workflows if this bucket's clusters become unavailable. Continue?`
When operator flips toggle OFF, no confirmation needed (relaxing)
Mode toggle does NOT require re-typing bucket name (less destructive than drain)
Bundle size delta ≤ 2 KiB gzipped
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Landed: BucketDetail Placement tab Strict toggle + strict header badge. Added PlacementMode wire type to api/placement.ts (BucketPlacement now {placement, mode}; setBucketPlacement gained optional mode arg; normalizeMode coerces empty/legacy to weighted). Added lightweight web/src/components/ui/switch.tsx (button[role=switch], aria-checked, ~150B gz — avoids @radix-ui/react-switch). BucketPlacementTab tracks mode in state, marks dirty on change, renders Switch row with title=tooltip explaining strict vs weighted, opens new strictConfirmOpen Dialog on flip ON (no confirm on flip OFF per AC), Save sends mode along with weights (always, so audit stamps admin:UpdateBucketPlacementMode). BucketDetailPage shares the placement query (same queryKey, no extra request) and passes placement to BucketBadges; badge renders only when bucket has policy AND mode==strict (nil-policy+strict is semantically weighted per EffectivePolicy). pnpm typecheck + build pass; bundle delta ~0.9 KiB gz (well under 2 KiB cap); pnpm test:unit (54 tests) pass. Manual browser verification deferred to US-006 e2e (no dev-browser MCP available in this environment).

userStories.title: BulkPlacementFixDialog filters to strict-flagged stuck buckets + per-bucket 'Flip to weighted' option
userStories.description: As an operator, I want the BulkPlacementFixDialog to focus on strict-flagged buckets only AND offer a 'flip to weighted' shortcut so I can resolve stuck buckets without manually editing policy clusters.
acceptanceCriteria: `<BulkPlacementFixDialog>` accepts `stuck` array; renders only entries where `bucket.PlacementMode == "strict"` (weighted stuck buckets auto-resolve via cluster weights — already non-stuck post US-003)
`Bulk fix N stuck buckets` CTA label updated to: `Fix N compliance-locked buckets` (explicit about why fix is needed)

Each row in dialog offers three actions: `Flip to weighted` (sets `mode=weighted` for this bucket; auto-resolves via cluster weights); `Replace cluster` (operator picks a live cluster from existing suggested-policies dropdown; keeps mode=strict); `Keep stuck` (leaves this bucket out of bulk-fix; drain remains blocked for this bucket until operator acts later)
Top-of-dialog `Apply uniform to all selected` toggle still works but now includes `Flip all to weighted` as one of the uniform options
On Apply: per-bucket PUT placement carrying operator's choice (mode + policy if changed); drain-impact cache invalidated; modal closes; parent refetches /drain-impact
`<ConfirmDrainModal>` `stuck` amber row label updated: `<N> compliance-locked buckets need fix` (instead of generic `<N> stuck buckets`)
When zero strict-stuck buckets remain → `stuck` row hides; Submit unlocked
Bundle size delta ≤ 3 KiB gzipped
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Landed: BulkPlacementFixDialog filters input stuck array to placement_mode=='strict' via new strictOnly helper. ConfirmDrainModal + BucketReferencesDrawer hand down the same compliance-locked subset (computed locally before invoking onOpenBulkFix). Server-side BucketImpactEntry gains `PlacementMode string` field populated via `meta.NormalizePlacementMode(b.PlacementMode)` so the UI can branch on bucket mode without joining a second endpoint; OpenAPI schema updated with the new required enum [weighted, strict]. SuggestedPolicy TS type already carried placement_mode_override (US-003 effective-placement); BulkPlacementFixDialog Apply now threads `resolveModeOverride(choice)` ∈ {weighted, strict} into setBucketPlacement so the server stamps `admin:UpdateBucketPlacementMode` audit on every fix. Apply CTA label: 'Fix N compliance-locked bucket(s)' (was 'Apply to N'). Dialog title + description rewritten around compliance-fix narrative (flip vs replace vs keep). ConfirmDrainModal stuck amber row label: 'N compliance-locked buckets need fix' (was 'N chunks across M buckets would be stranded'); Submit blocks on complianceLockedCount (was stuckTotal) — when zero strict-stuck buckets remain the amber row hides and Submit unlocks per PRD. BucketReferencesDrawer button: 'Fix N compliance-locked bucket(s)' (was 'Bulk fix N stuck buckets'). 'Apply uniform to all selected' toggle inherits Flip-to-weighted naturally because the server emits identical 'Flip to weighted (auto-fallback to cluster weights)' labels on every strict-stuck bucket → it lives in the intersection. New unit tests strictOnly + resolveModeOverride (3 cases) in bulk-placement-fix-dialog.test.mjs (57 total pass). Server-side TestClusterDrainImpact_CategorizesAndSorts now asserts the populated placement_mode field. pnpm typecheck + build + test:unit pass; go vet + go build + go test ./internal/adminapi/ pass. Two flakes (TestRunSmokeBinary, TestThreeReplicaDistribution) trip under parallel suite load but both pass in isolation — unrelated to this story (touches neither cmd/strata-racecheck nor internal/lifecycle).

userStories.title: E2E smoke + Playwright + docs + ROADMAP close-flip + PRD removal
userStories.description: As an operator and as a future-maintainer, I need every fix validated end-to-end against the multi-cluster lab + documented so I trust the cycle is actually done.
acceptanceCriteria: `scripts/smoke-effective-placement.sh` covers the 12-step walkthrough end-to-end against `docker compose --profile multi-cluster`

Scenario A (weighted auto-fallback): seed bucket-B with `Placement={cephb:1}` mode=weighted; drain cephb evacuate; /drain-impact reports migratable_chunks>0 + stuck_single_policy=0; worker migrates B's chunks to default; /drain-progress eventually reports deregister_ready=true (assuming no strict buckets)

Scenario B (strict blocks drain): seed bucket-C with `Placement={cephb:1}` mode=strict; drain cephb evacuate; /drain-impact reports stuck_single_policy=1; drain Submit returns 409 `stuck buckets exist`; manual PUT placement on bucket-C → after invalidation /drain-impact reports stuck=0; drain succeeds

Scenario C (flip strict→weighted resolves stuck): seed bucket-C with mode=strict; drain cephb; observe stuck=1; PUT /buckets/bucket-C/placement {placement: {cephb:1}, mode: "weighted"}; /drain-impact reports stuck=0 immediately; drain Submit succeeds
Scenario D (all clusters drained → 503): drain default first then cephb; both in evacuate mode; PUT to bucket-D (no Placement, mode=weighted) → cluster weights also empty → 503 DrainRefused
Per-scenario log lines via `echo "==> Scenario X: ..."`
Script EXITS NON-ZERO on any assertion failure
`make smoke-effective-placement` Makefile target wraps the script

Playwright spec `web/e2e/effective-placement.spec.ts` covers UI half: BucketDetail toggle (off→on with confirm, on→off no confirm); BulkPlacementFixDialog filter (only strict buckets shown); ConfirmDrainModal stuck-row label updated to `compliance-locked`; drain workflow with mixed strict/weighted buckets

`docs/site/content/best-practices/placement-rebalance.md` gains new `Strict vs Weighted placement` section: when to use strict (data-sovereignty, replication design, hot/cold separation), when to use weighted (typical operator deploys, auto-fallback friendly), migration note (existing buckets get mode=weighted by default; no operator action needed)
`docs/site/content/best-practices/web-ui.md` capability matrix gains rows for: Strict placement toggle, `Flip to weighted` bulk-fix action, Compliance-locked bucket badge
Project root `CLAUDE.md` `Cluster state machine` section gets a note about the new mode field + EffectivePolicy resolution order

`ROADMAP.md` close-flip the P2 entry → `~~**P2 — Effective-policy fallback to cluster weights — eliminate forced Placement edit on drain.**~~ — **Done.** <one-line summary>. (commit \`<pending>\`)`; closing SHA backfilled in follow-up commit on main
`tasks/prd-effective-placement.md` REMOVED in the same commit (PRD lifecycle rule)
Manual screenshots: BucketDetail toggle on/off states; BulkPlacementFixDialog filtered view (only strict buckets); /drain-impact JSON before/after showing stuck_single_policy count drop
`make docs-build` succeeds
`make vet` succeeds
`make test` succeeds
`pnpm run build` succeeds
`make smoke-effective-placement` succeeds against the running multi-cluster lab
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Landed: scripts/smoke-effective-placement.sh covers the four operator scenarios (A weighted-auto-fallback via cluster.weights, B strict pins stuck_single_policy + 503 DrainRefused on mid-drain PUT + deregister_ready stays false, C flip strict→weighted via PUT placement clears stuck via cache invalidation + drain converges, D drain both clusters → nil-policy PUT returns 503 DrainRefused) end-to-end against the multi-cluster compose profile. Per-scenario banner via `echo ==> Scenario X:`; cleanup trap best-effort-undrains both clusters + recursive-deletes spawned buckets. Skips with exit 77 when lab is not reachable (REQUIRE_LAB=1 converts to hard fail). Makefile target `smoke-effective-placement` wraps the script with the canonical comment block. Playwright spec `web/e2e/effective-placement.spec.ts` covers the UI half in four scenarios (A BucketDetail Strict switch off→on with confirm + Save sends mode=strict + header badge mounts + on→off one-click no-confirm; B BulkPlacementFixDialog strictOnly filter hides weighted stuck rows; C per-bucket Flip-to-weighted Apply submits mode=weighted in PUT body + parent modal refetches /drain-impact unlocking Submit; D ConfirmDrainModal amber row 'compliance-locked' + Submit blocks while count>0 with typed-confirm matching). All admin endpoints spoofed via page.route() so the spec runs against the memory-mode webServer. Docs: docs/site/content/best-practices/placement-rebalance.md gains a 'Strict vs Weighted placement (per-bucket mode)' section with the 6-row truth table + when-to-use guidance + migration note (existing buckets default to weighted, no operator action) + console-surface summary. docs/site/content/best-practices/web-ui.md capability matrix gains 5 new rows (Strict toggle, compliance-locked header badge, BulkPlacementFixDialog filter, Flip-to-weighted bulk action, ConfirmDrainModal Submit gating) + a full e2e entry for effective-placement.spec.ts in the test catalogue. Project root CLAUDE.md 'Cluster state machine' section gains a `PlacementMode` block documenting NormalizePlacementMode, EffectivePolicy resolution order (live-bucket-subset > strict-refuse > weighted-fallback), `mode==""` legacy coercion, nil-policy+strict treated-as-weighted boundary, admin audit `admin:UpdateBucketPlacementMode`. ROADMAP P2 entry close-flipped with one-line summary referencing all 6 stories + commit `<pending>` slot (closing SHA backfilled in follow-up commit per CLAUDE.md PRD lifecycle rule). tasks/prd-effective-placement.md removed (Ralph snapshot at scripts/ralph/archive/<date>-<branch>/{prd.json,progress.txt} is the canonical record once archive_cycle runs). Quality: `make vet` passes, `go build ./...` passes, `go test ./...` passes (all packages cached/green; no flakes triggered this run), `pnpm typecheck` passes (tsc -b --noEmit clean), `pnpm build` passes (Storage chunk 63.62KB → 15.84KB gz; index 695.61KB → 191.71KB gz unchanged from US-005), `pnpm test:unit` (57 tests) passes, `make docs-build` passes (Hugo 59 pages built in 148ms). `make smoke-effective-placement` is gated on the running multi-cluster lab; operator runs it via `docker compose --profile multi-cluster up -d && make smoke-effective-placement` per the docs runbook (the script auto-skips with exit 77 if the lab isn't reachable). Manual browser screenshots deferred — no dev-browser MCP wired in this environment; Playwright spec is the canonical UI regression gate.
